data.body: PASS 1 (E4 — assumption/premise verification) for Cluster 1: probed every codebase assertion in the plan via Read/Grep. All material premises verified TRUE: (a) `_store/canonical.py` is the canonical seam with `canonical_str`/`canonical_bytes` using `ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")` [canonical.py:39-47]; (b) 6 production importers of the seam exist (event_append, inbound_translate, batch_dispatch, delete, compact, txn); (c) `signing.py:_canonical_payload` already uses the canonical kwargs → byte-identical [signing.py:187-199]; (d) `schema.py` `canonical_json`/`content_hash` re-exported in `llm/workflow/__init__.py.__all__` [__init__.py:56-57,85-86]; (e) `conflict_resolver._hash_value` uses `default=str` [conflict_resolver.py:217-219]; (f) `mutation.serialize_manifest` uses `ensure_ascii=True` [mutation.py:106]; (g) byte-parity contract test at tests/unit/test_canonical.py with its structural guard scanning src for raw json.dumps on events. No unverifiable/false premises found for E4. One adjacency note (NOT an E4 gap): test_canonical.py's structural guard scans `src/rebar` for raw `json.dumps` of event dicts — implementers should confirm the migrated mutation.py path does not trip it.
